task_completion.description: Agent enters KSJC (San Jose) as the starting waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'KSJC' inputted into the origin or first waypoint field of a flight planning interface.

task_completion.description: Agent enters KSMF (Sacramento) as the second waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'KSMF' inputted as the second waypoint in the route.

task_completion.description: Agent enters KRNO (Reno) as the third waypoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show 'KRNO' inputted as the third waypoint or destination in the route.

task_completion.description: The outbound flight plan is successfully created with the correct sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The flight planning application displays the active route sequence exactly as: KSJC -> KSMF -> KRNO.

task_completion.description: The flight plan is reversed for the return trip.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The flight planning application displays the final active route sequence exactly as: KRNO -> KSMF -> KSJC.

integrity.description: Agent used a genuine flight planning application or map interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a flight planner, aviation map, or flight simulator interface, not a terminal or command prompt.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply type the answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The waypoints and routes are entered into designated fields of a flight planning tool, not a blank text document like Notepad or TextEdit.

integrity.description: The route reversal was performed within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The reversed route (KRNO -> KSMF -> KSJC) is shown as an active route or flight plan list within the application's UI, not just printed as text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The outbound flight plan must consist of the waypoints KSJC (San Jose), KSMF (Sacramento), and KRNO (Reno) in that exact order. The final, reversed flight plan must show the active sequence as KRNO -> KSMF -> KSJC.
